title: Geographical Features in News
focus: Key international geographical features including straits, biomes, peninsulas, glaciers, and shoals recently in the news.
rationale: Covers diverse geographical locations like Davis Strait, Kamchatka Peninsula, and Drake Passage, which are highly testable for UPSC map-based questions.
sectionExcerpt: Davis Strait Proto-Microcontinent: Located beneath the Davis Strait (connects Labrador Sea and Baffin Bay)... Arctic Biome: North of the Arctic Circle... Kamchatka Peninsula: Russian Far East on the Pacific Ring of Fire... Perito Moreno glacier: Los Glaciares National Park in Argentina... Drake Passage: Between Cape Horn and South Shetland Islands... Scarborough Shoal: A small atoll in the South China Sea.
evidenceBullets: Davis Strait connects the Labrador Sea and Baffin Bay.
Kamchatka Peninsula is located in the Russian Far East on the Pacific Ring of Fire, surrounded by the Bering Sea, Pacific Ocean, and Sea of Okhotsk.
Perito Moreno glacier, known as the 'White Giant', is located in Argentina's Los Glaciares National Park.
Drake Passage lies between Cape Horn and the South Shetland Islands, forming part of the Antarctic Circumpolar Current.
Scarborough Shoal (Huangyan Island/Panatag Shoal) is a small atoll in the South China Sea.

title: Rivers and Water Bodies in News
focus: Major international rivers, lakes, and seas in the news due to environmental changes or infrastructure projects.
rationale: Rivers like the Congo and Blue Nile, and drying water bodies like the Aral Sea, are classic UPSC geography and environment topics.
sectionExcerpt: Tulare Lake: San Joaquin Valley, California... Aral Sea: Boundary between Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan... Congo River: River Basin covers 9 countries... deepest river in the world and crosses equator twice... Blue Nile: Origin: lake Tana of Ethiopia... Great Ruaha River: Important tributary of Rufiji River... Seine River: Second longest river in France after Loire.
evidenceBullets: The Aral Sea is located on the boundary between Kazakhstan (North) and Uzbekistan (South).
The Congo River crosses the equator twice, is the deepest river in the world, and its basin holds the world's largest tropical peatland.
The Blue Nile originates from Lake Tana in Ethiopia and meets the White Nile at Khartoum to form the Nile.
The Great Ruaha River is a major tributary of the Rufiji River in Tanzania.
The Seine River flows through Paris and empties into the English Channel at Le Havre.

title: Disaster Affected Areas in News
focus: Global locations recently affected by natural disasters such as volcanic eruptions, earthquakes, cyclones, and floods.
rationale: Matching volcanoes and disaster zones to their respective countries or regions is a frequent pattern in UPSC prelims.
sectionExcerpt: Mount Etna (Italy): Volcanic Eruption. Located in Sicily Island... Cyclone Asna: Tropical cyclone along the west coast, Arabian Sea... Mount Lewotobi Laki-laki (Indonesia): Volcanic Eruption. Located in Flores Island... Kilauea volcano (Hawaii, U.S.)... Hayli Gubbi (Ethiopia)... Mount Semeru (Indonesia): Highest volcano on Java Island.
evidenceBullets: Mount Etna is Europe's largest active volcano, located on Sicily Island, Italy.
Mount Lewotobi Laki-laki is a volcano located on Flores Island, Indonesia.
Kilauea volcano is situated on the southeastern part of the island of Hawaii.
Mount Semeru is a stratovolcano and the highest volcano on Java Island, Indonesia.
Hayli Gubbi is a volcano located in Ethiopia.

title: Countries in News
focus: Countries highlighted for significant environmental treaties, summits, or geopolitical developments.
rationale: International summits, treaties (like the Falepili Union), and environmental initiatives linked to specific countries are highly relevant for current affairs.
sectionExcerpt: Vanuatu: Approached the ICJ to classify environmental destruction as ecocide... Colombia: Second WHO Global Conference on Air Pollution and Health held in Cartagena... Denmark: India and Denmark reaffirm Green Strategic Partnership... Ethiopia: UN Food Systems Summit +4 Stocktake held in Addis Ababa... Tuvalu: Signed the 2023 Falepili Union Treaty with Australia... Ecuador: Indigenous groups criticize oil drilling expansion in the Amazon.
evidenceBullets: Vanuatu approached the International Court of Justice to classify environmental destruction as ecocide.
Colombia co-organized the Second WHO Global Conference on Air Pollution and Health in Cartagena.
Tuvalu signed the 2023 Falepili Union Treaty with Australia due to threats from rising sea levels.
Ethiopia hosted the UN Food Systems Summit +4 Stocktake in Addis Ababa.
